signals.title: USD — FOMC Decision + Warsh Debut
signals.text: Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) vs ECB 2.25% (HIKING) — 150bp carry advantage favors USD as the FOMC prepares to announce its first decision under Chairman Kevin Warsh. USD CESI net +25 (strong positive) confirms a resilient macro backdrop supporting the 'higher-for-longer' stance despite the ECB's hiking cycle. Market pricing remains centered on a hold, shifting focus entirely to the post-meeting press conference for Warsh's policy lean. USD/EUR carry remains the structural floor for DXY at 99.69.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.20 — price is holding above the 160.00 psychological level that previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite the BoJ hiking to 1.0%, the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage continues to drive JPY weakness. Options market is aggressively hedging for a reversal with 25d RR at -1.09 (moderate put skew) and IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 3.3% (VRP +3.9%). The elevated vol premium and put skew signal institutional preparation for BoJ intervention or a carry unwind if the FOMC surprises dovish.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CPI Miss + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3397 — soft UK CPI data has clipped BoE hike bets, yet COT LF net remains long +21,961 (61.7% long). This creates a long-unwind risk as the macro data (GBP CESI net 0) fails to support the crowded speculative long position. Options market aligns with the downside risk, showing a 25d RR of -0.67 (put skew) and an elevated VRP of +5.1% (IV 11.0% vs HV30 5.9%). Failure to hold 1.3400 targets the 1.3350 support zone.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail vs Institutional Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5818 — retail sentiment is 85% long (extreme) while COT LF net is short -22,354 (specs fading NZD). This extreme divergence at the 0.5800 handle suggests retail is attempting to catch a bottom while institutions remain positioned for further downside, supported by a negative NZD CESI (-4). Retail capitulation risk is high if the FOMC delivers a hawkish hold, potentially driving a break toward 0.5750.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4029 — the pair is decoupling from energy prices with USD/CAD vs WTI correlation at -0.354 (z = -1.64σ). The 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) is currently the primary driver, overriding the 0.49% intraday gain in WTI oil. CAD remains the G10 laggard (1W avg -0.424%) as speculative shorts (COT net -60,671) remain crowded. Watch for a sustained break above 1.4050 to confirm the carry-dominant regime.
